company.name: GitLab
company.notes: DevOps. Hires anywhere. Less creative, analyst angle possible.

research.status_summary: GitLab is undergoing active restructuring layoffs — cutting ~14% of staff (~350 employees) as of June 3, 2026, following a May 2026 'Act 2' announcement to exit ~22 countries and flatten management, while selectively reopening remote roles globally.
research.hiring_policy: GitLab is all-remote since inception with team members in 65+ countries, but is actively shrinking its country footprint by ~30% (exiting ~18-22 countries); it hires via local entities and historically files minimal H-1B LCAs (0 in FY2025), making it more EOR/contractor-friendly than visa-sponsorship-friendly for non-US candidates.
recent_news: June 3, 2026 (TechCrunch): GitLab confirmed ~14% workforce reduction (~350 employees) on its Q1 FY2027 earnings call, with $30–35M in restructuring charges expected.
Q1 FY2027 revenue hit $264M (+23% YoY) with 88% gross margins, but FY2027 full-year guidance of $1.10–1.12B implies a deceleration to ~15-17% growth — down from 26% in FY2026.

May 11, 2026 ('GitLab Act 2'): CEO Bill Staples announced R&D reorganization into ~60 autonomous teams, removal of up to 3 management layers, and a strategic pivot to agentic AI — stock dropped ~8% after hours, extending a 12-month slide from $52 to ~$26.
GitLab Duo (its AI coding platform) only reached general availability on January 15, 2026, putting it behind GitHub Copilot (20M users) and Cursor ($2B ARR) in the AI dev tools race.
GitLab crossed $1B ARR and $955M revenue in FY2026 (+26% YoY), with free cash flow up 80%+ to ~$220M — financially solid despite the restructuring optics.
research.growth_signal: Revenue growing 23% YoY to $264M in Q1 FY2027 with 88% gross margins, but headcount is being cut and country footprint reduced, signaling a profitability-over-growth phase.
research.fit_for_lior: Strong analyst-angle fit — GitLab is a publicly traded DevSecOps platform (NASDAQ: GTLB) in mid-transformation, with open Data Analyst, FP&A analytics, and platform product roles that suit a structured, data-literate profile; creative AI or vibe-coding threads are weak here, but DevOps tooling and agentic-AI product analysis are genuinely relevant.
